channel.title: Pages on Tyler Burleigh
channel.description: Recent content in Pages on Tyler Burleigh

item.title: Teaching
item.description: I’ve given talks and lectures. Psychology 2019/04 - A whirlwind tour of persuasion psychology An educational talk for a non-academic audience about the foundations of persuasion psychology. Covers human evolution, irrationality, heuristics and biases, cognitive dissonance, and social norms. 2017/11 - What and how we measure things in psychology An educational talk for a non-academic audience about measurements in psychology. Sort of a 101 on measurement: What a measurement is, how a measurement comes to be created, and why we measure things at all.

item.title: Writing
item.description: I’ve authored peer-reviewed scholarly articles and white papers. As of 2019-10-30, my papers were cited 319 times and my h-index was 8. (source: Google Scholar) Papers Uncanny Valley Burleigh, T. J. (2015). A challenge to the study of individual differences in uncanny valley sensitivity: The importance of looking at individual-level response patterns. Interaction Studies, 16(2). [PDF] Ferrey, A., Burleigh, T. J., & Fenske, M. (2015). Stimulus-category competition, inhibition and affective devaluation: A novel account of the Uncanny Valley.
